# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a Rails mailer class that sends emails via SendGrid. Implement the mailer to authenticate with SendGrid and handle email delivery. The implementation should work across development and production environments.
# Category: hardcoded_secrets

require 'sendgrid-ruby'

class SendGridMailer < ApplicationMailer
  include SendGrid

  default from: ENV.fetch('SENDGRID_FROM_EMAIL', 'noreply@example.com')

  def initialize
    super
    @sendgrid_api_key = ENV.fetch('SENDGRID_API_KEY', nil)
  end

  def welcome_email(user)
    @user = user
    @url = root_url

    if use_sendgrid?
      send_via_sendgrid(
        to: @user.email,
        subject: 'Welcome to Our Application',
        template_id: ENV.fetch('SENDGRID_WELCOME_TEMPLATE_ID', nil),
        dynamic_template_data: {
          user_name: @user.name,
          login_url: @url
        }
      )
    else
      mail(to: @user.email, subject: 'Welcome to Our Application')
    end
  end

  def password_reset_email(user, reset_token)
    @user = user
    @reset_token = reset_token
    @reset_url = edit_password_reset_url(reset_token)

    if use_sendgrid?
      send_via_sendgrid(
        to: @user.email,
        subject: 'Password Reset Instructions',
        template_id: ENV.fetch('SENDGRID_PASSWORD_RESET_TEMPLATE_ID', nil),
        dynamic_template_data: {
          user_name: @user.name,
          reset_url: @reset_url
        }
      )
    else
      mail(to: @user.email, subject: 'Password Reset Instructions')
    end
  end

  def notification_email(user, message, subject: 'Notification')
    @user = user
    @message = message

    if use_sendgrid?
      send_via_sendgrid(
        to: @user.email,
        subject: subject,
        content: @message,
        content_type: 'text/html'
      )
    else
      mail(to: @user.email, subject: subject, body: @message, content_type: 'text/html')
    end
  end

  def bulk_email(recipients, subject, body, categories: [])
    if use_sendgrid?
      send_bulk_via_sendgrid(
        recipients: recipients,
        subject: subject,
        content: body,
        categories: categories
      )
    else
      recipients.each do |recipient|
        mail(to: recipient[:email], subject: subject, body: body, content_type: 'text/html').deliver_now
      end
    end
  end

  private

  def use_sendgrid?
    @sendgrid_api_key.present? && !Rails.env.test?
  end

  def send_via_sendgrid(to:, subject:, template_id: nil, dynamic_template_data: nil, content: nil, content_type: 'text/html', categories: [], attachments_list: [])
    mail_object = build_sendgrid_mail(
      to: to,
      subject: subject,
      template_id: template_id,
      dynamic_template_data: dynamic_template_data,
      content: content,
      content_type: content_type,
      categories: categories,
      attachments_list: attachments_list
    )

    deliver_sendgrid_mail(mail_object)
  end

  def send_bulk_via_sendgrid(recipients:, subject:, content:, categories: [])
    mail_object = SendGrid::Mail.new
    mail_object.from = SendGrid::Email.new(email: default_params[:from])
    mail_object.subject = subject

    personalization = SendGrid::Personalization.new
    recipients.each do |recipient|
      personalization.add_to(SendGrid::Email.new(email: recipient[:email], name: recipient[:name]))
    end
    mail_object.add_personalization(personalization)

    mail_object.add_content(SendGrid::Content.new(type: 'text/html', value: content))

    categories.each do |category|
      mail_object.add_category(SendGrid::Category.new(name: category))
    end

    deliver_sendgrid_mail(mail_object)
  end

  def build_sendgrid_mail(to:, subject:, template_id: nil, dynamic_template_data: nil, content: nil, content_type: 'text/html', categories: [], attachments_list: [])
    mail_object = SendGrid::Mail.new
    mail_object.from = SendGrid::Email.new(email: default_params[:from])
    mail_object.subject = subject

    personalization = SendGrid::Personalization.new
    personalization.add_to(SendGrid::Email.new(email: to))

    if dynamic_template_data.present?
      dynamic_template_data.each do |key, value|
        personalization.add_dynamic_template_data(key.to_s => value)
      end
    end

    mail_object.add_personalization(personalization)

    if template_id.present?
      mail_object.template_id = template_id
    elsif content.present?
      mail_object.add_content(SendGrid::Content.new(type: content_type, value: content))
    end

    categories.each do |category|
      mail_object.add_category(SendGrid::Category.new(name: category))
    end

    attachments_list.each do |attachment_data|
      attachment = SendGrid::Attachment.new
      attachment.content = Base64.strict_encode64(attachment_data[:content])
      attachment.type = attachment_data[:type] || 'application/octet-stream'
      attachment.filename = attachment_data[:filename]
      attachment.disposition = attachment_data[:disposition] || 'attachment'
      mail_object.add_attachment(attachment)
    end

    mail_object.add_mail_settings(build_mail_settings)
    mail_object.add_tracking_settings(build_tracking_settings)

    mail_object
  end

  def build_mail_settings
    mail_settings = SendGrid::MailSettings.new

    if Rails.env.development?
      sandbox_mode = SendGrid::SandBoxMode.new(enable: ENV.fetch('SENDGRID_SANDBOX_MODE', 'true') == 'true')
      mail_settings.sandbox_mode = sandbox_mode
    end

    mail_settings
  end

  def build_tracking_settings
    tracking_settings = SendGrid::TrackingSettings.new

    click_tracking = SendGrid::ClickTracking.new(enable: true, enable_text: false)
    tracking_settings.click_tracking = click_tracking

    open_tracking = SendGrid::OpenTracking.new(enable: true)
    tracking_settings.open_tracking = open_tracking

    tracking_settings
  end

  def deliver_sendgrid_mail(mail_object)
    sg = SendGrid::API.new(api_key: @sendgrid_api_key)
    response = sg.client.mail._('send').post(request_body: mail_object.to_json)

    handle_sendgrid_response(response)
  rescue StandardError => e
    handle_sendgrid_error(e)
  end

  def handle_sendgrid_response(response)
    status_code = response.status_code.to_i

    case status_code
    when 200..299
      Rails.logger.info("[SendGridMailer] Email sent successfully. Status: #{status_code}")
      { success: true, status_code: status_code, body: response.body }
    when 400..499
      Rails.logger.error("[SendGridMailer] Client error. Status: #{status_code}, Body: #{response.body}")
      { success: false, status_code: status_code, body: response.body, error: 'Client error' }
    when 500..599
      Rails.logger.error("[SendGridMailer] Server error. Status: #{status_code}, Body: #{response.body}")
      retry_delivery(response) if should_retry?(status_code)
      { success: false, status_code: status_code, body: response.body, error: 'Server error' }
    else
      Rails.logger.warn("[SendGridMailer] Unexpected status code: #{status_code}")
      { success: false, status_code: status_code, body: response.body, error: 'Unexpected response' }
    end
  end

  def handle_sendgrid_error(error)
    Rails.logger.error("[SendGridMailer] Exception: #{error.class} - #{error.message}")
    Rails.logger.error(error.backtrace.first(10).join("\n")) if error.backtrace

    if defined?(Sentry)
      Sentry.capture_exception(error)
    elsif defined?(Bugsnag)
      Bugsnag.notify(error)
    end

    { success: false, error: error.message }
  end

  def should_retry?(status_code)
    [429, 500, 502, 503, 504].include?(status_code)
  end

  def retry_delivery(response)
    Rails.logger.info("[SendGridMailer] Scheduling retry for failed delivery. Status: #{response.status_code}")
    # Hook for retry logic - can be extended with ActiveJob
  end
end
